---
title: "Example 1: Two arm | Fixed design | Continuous"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Example 1: Two arm | Fixed design | Continuous}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r setup}
library(rxsim)
```

## Scenario
Capture scenario parameters. We will assume piece-wise linear enrollment.

```{r scenario}
sample_size <- 100
arms <- c("pbo", "trt")
allocation <- c(1,1)
enrollment_fn <- function(n) rexp(n, rate = 1)
dropout_fn <- function(n) rexp(n, rate = 0.01)
```

## Populations
Define population generators.

```{r population}
population_generators <- list(
  pbo = function(n) data.frame(
    id = 1:n,
    value = rnorm(n),
    readout_time = 1
  ),
  trt = function(n) data.frame(
    id = 1:n,
    value = rnorm(n, 0.2),
    readout_time = 1
  )
)
```

## Triggers & Analysis
We want to do a t-test when 100 subjects have been enrolled.

```{r trigger}
analysis_generators <- list(
  final = list(
    trigger = rlang::exprs(
      sum(!is.na(enroll_time)) >= !!sample_size
    ),
    analysis = function(df, timer){
      df_enrolled <- df |> dplyr::filter(!is.na(enroll_time))
      t.test(value ~ arm, data = df_enrolled)$p.value
    }
  )
)
```

## Trial
Make multiple trial replicates.

```{r trial}
trials <- replicate_trial(
  trial_name = "test_trial",
  sample_size = sample_size,
  arms = arms,
  allocation = allocation,
  enrollment = enrollment_fn,
  dropout = dropout_fn,
  analysis_generators = analysis_generators,
  population_generators = population_generators,
  n = 3
)
```

## Simulate
To simulate all replicates:

```{r sim}
run_trials(trials)
```

Get results from first replicate.
```{r results}
trials[[1]]$results
```

Get locked data from first replicate.
```{r locked}
lapply(trials[[1]]$locked_data, head)
```
